title: Deepen position lifecycle module across day, swing, and expiry flows
description:
## Problem
Position open/close/risk/expiry logic is repeated across modules and jobs. The current seam is shallow, with similar orchestration copied in multiple implementations.

## Proposed change
Create one deep position lifecycle module that owns close semantics, emergency handling, and shared risk/expiry close pipeline. Keep day/swing/expiry policy modules as thin adapters at the seam.

## Why this matters
- Improves **locality**: lifecycle behavior and edge-case handling live in one module.
- Increases **leverage**: multiple strategies reuse one robust implementation.
- Raises **depth** by concentrating complex behavior behind a smaller interface.
- Improves testability via focused lifecycle scenario tests.

## Files
- `app/models/position.rb`
- `app/services/trading/day_trading_position_manager.rb`
- `app/services/trading/swing_position_manager.rb`
- `app/services/contract_expiry_manager.rb`
- `app/jobs/position_close_job.rb`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Shared lifecycle implementation is used across day/swing/expiry paths.
- [ ] Duplicate close/reason/error logic is removed from policy adapters.
- [ ] Lifecycle tests cover normal, risk-triggered, and expiry-triggered closure.

title: Create deep Coinbase adapter seam for trading and monitoring
description:
## Problem
Coinbase integration modules leak implementation details across seams (including private/reflection usage from callers). Authentication and request behavior are duplicated across adapters, reducing module depth.

## Proposed change
Introduce one deep Coinbase adapter module with explicit interface for auth status, balances, positions, margin window state, and market prices. Move HTTP/JWT/private request implementation behind that seam.

## Why this matters
- Improves **locality**: Coinbase API behavior and error handling in one module.
- Increases **leverage**: trading/monitoring callers rely on one stable interface.
- Raises **depth**: less pass-through logic and less seam leakage.
- Improves testability with adapter contract tests.

## Files
- `app/services/trading/coinbase_positions.rb`
- `app/services/coinbase/advanced_trade_client.rb`
- `app/services/coinbase/exchange_client.rb`
- `app/services/coinbase/client.rb`
- `app/services/trading/swing_position_manager.rb`
- `app/jobs/margin_window_monitoring_job.rb`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Callers no longer access private Coinbase client implementation details.
- [ ] One adapter interface is used for trading + monitoring flows.
- [ ] Contract tests verify auth, positions, balances, and margin window behavior.

title: Deepen signal API module by removing duplicate controller seam
description:
## Problem
Two modules (`SignalController` and `SignalsController`) expose nearly identical interface and implementation for signal endpoints. This creates a shallow seam and behavior drift risk.

## Proposed change
Consolidate to one canonical signal API module and remove the duplicate controller path. Keep one interface for filtering, validation, and signal evaluation.

## Why this matters
- Improves **locality**: signal endpoint behavior changes in one module.
- Increases **leverage**: callers use one consistent interface.
- Raises **depth**: less duplicated controller implementation.
- Simplifies test surface for request/controller specs.

## Files
- `app/controllers/signal_controller.rb`
- `app/controllers/signals_controller.rb`
- `config/routes.rb`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Only one signal controller module remains in active routes.
- [ ] Signal index/show/health behavior remains backward compatible.
- [ ] Request specs cover the consolidated interface.
close_reason: Removed duplicate SignalsController; canonical SignalController remains; signal request/controller specs passing.

title: Wire GenerateSignalsJob to FuturesExecutor for real order placement
description: GenerateSignalsJob evaluates strategy signals but never executes them — it only logs. The executor path is stubbed with a TODO comment. Need to call FuturesExecutor#consider_entry (or a new place_order method) when a signal fires, with appropriate guards (paper-trading mode flag, kill switch, position limits).

title: Add kill switch / trading halt mechanism
description: No global kill switch exists. Monitoring jobs (margin, basis, expiry) log warnings but cannot halt new order placement. Need a lightweight on/off gate (e.g. a Rails.cache flag, a DB column on a Settings model, or an ENV override) that FuturesExecutor and trading services check before placing any order. Should be settable via rake task and/or CLI command.

title: Merge feat/tui-position-management branch to main
description: The TUI position management branch contains several sessions of work (import, close, reconcile from dashboard, escape-safe input, arrow shortcuts, startup sync refactor, side normalizer, realtime signal runner) and the test suite is green. Run parallel_rspec, pass StandardRB and Brakeman, then open PR and merge.

title: Unify futures contract semantics behind one deep module seam
description:
## Problem
Contract parsing, asset mapping, and expiry semantics are implemented in multiple modules. Callers know too much about contract string details, which leaks implementation across seams.

## Proposed change
Establish one deep contract-domain module that owns parsing, asset resolution, expiry math, and current/upcoming contract selection semantics.

## Why this matters
- Improves **locality**: contract rule changes occur in one module.
- Increases **leverage**: execution, market-data, and position modules share one interface.
- Raises **depth**: hides regex/string implementation behind a seam.
- Improves testability with one focused contract behavior suite.

## Files
- `app/services/futures_contract.rb`
- `app/services/market_data/futures_contract_manager.rb`
- `app/services/execution/futures_executor.rb`
- `app/services/trading/coinbase_positions.rb`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] One contract semantics interface is used by execution/market/trading modules.
- [ ] Duplicate parsing/mapping logic is removed from callers.
- [ ] Contract behavior tests cover parsing, rollover, and expiry edge cases.

title: Refactor position management jobs into deep orchestration modules
description:
## Problem
Several management jobs mix scheduling triggers with domain implementation and side effects (alerts/monitoring). This creates shallow modules and repetitive orchestration seams.

## Proposed change
Move workflow implementation into deep orchestration modules per flow (day, swing, expiry). Keep ActiveJob classes as minimal trigger adapters.

## Why this matters
- Improves **locality**: workflow logic is centralized outside job shells.
- Increases **leverage**: same orchestration can run from jobs, rake tasks, or manual operations.
- Raises **depth** by reducing per-job custom implementation.
- Enables deterministic orchestration tests without heavy job harness setup.

## Files
- `app/jobs/day_trading_position_management_job.rb`
- `app/jobs/end_of_day_position_closure_job.rb`
- `app/jobs/swing_position_management_job.rb`
- `app/jobs/swing_risk_monitoring_job.rb`
- `app/jobs/contract_expiry_monitoring_job.rb`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Job modules are thin trigger adapters.
- [ ] Domain orchestration sits in dedicated deep modules.
- [ ] Shared alert/error policy is reusable across flows.

title: Add 'bin/futuresbot start' all-in-one launch command
description:
## Summary
Add a single 'start' command to bin/futuresbot that launches the TUI dashboard, starts the market data feed, and starts the realtime signal evaluation loop in a coordinated way.

## Why
Currently an operator must run bin/futuresbot dashboard, bin/rake market_data:subscribe, and bin/rake realtime:signals separately. A single entry point reduces friction and makes the primary-interface story complete.

## Scope
- Add 'start' Thor command to FuturesBotCli
- Start MarketDataSubscribeJob (or inline subscriber) in background thread/process
- Start RealtimeSignalRunner in background thread
- Launch TUI dashboard in foreground
- On dashboard exit, cleanly shut down background threads
- Show startup status in TUI flash/header before first render
- Add spec coverage for the command wiring (not the background threads themselves)

## Acceptance Criteria
- bin/futuresbot start launches all three subsystems
- Ctrl+C / q in TUI shuts down cleanly
- FUTURESBOT_SKIP_POSITION_SYNC respected on start
- bin/standardrb passes

title: Add TUI halt/resume key and market+signal status to dashboard
description:
## Summary
Add a kill-switch toggle to the TUI dashboard and surface market data / signal evaluation state in the status row.

## Why
The TUI is the primary interface but currently requires leaving the screen to halt trading or check if the signal loop / market feed is running.

## Scope
- Add 'h' / 'H' key to toggle TradingHalt on/off with a confirmation prompt (same pattern as close/reconcile)
- Add trading halt indicator to status row (🟢 ACTIVE / 🔴 HALTED)
- Add market feed status to status row (LIVE tick age already shown; make halt state explicit)
- Add signal eval last-run timestamp to status row
- Update key binding hint line in header

## Acceptance Criteria
- 'h' key prompts confirmation then halts/resumes trading and shows flash message
- Status row shows halt state at a glance
- Specs cover new key binding and status row rendering
- bin/standardrb passes

title: Add 'bin/futuresbot start' all-in-one launch command
description:
## Summary
Add a single `start` command to bin/futuresbot that launches the TUI dashboard, starts the market data feed, and starts the realtime signal evaluation loop in a coordinated way.

## Why
Currently an operator must run `bin/futuresbot dashboard`, `bin/rake market_data:subscribe`, and `bin/rake realtime:signals` separately. A single entry point reduces friction and makes the primary-interface story complete.

## Scope
- Add `start` Thor command to FuturesBotCli
- Start MarketDataSubscribeJob / inline subscriber in background thread
- Start RealtimeSignalRunner in background thread
- Launch TUI dashboard in foreground
- On dashboard exit, cleanly shut down background threads
- Show startup status in TUI flash/header before first render
- Respect `FUTURESBOT_SKIP_POSITION_SYNC`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `bin/futuresbot start` launches all three subsystems
- Ctrl+C / q in TUI shuts down cleanly
- bin/standardrb passes

Depends on: TUI halt key issue
Tracks bd issue: coinbase_futures_bot-2ln

title: Add TUI halt/resume key and market+signal status to dashboard
description:
## Summary
Add a kill-switch toggle to the TUI dashboard and surface market data / signal evaluation state in the status row.

## Why
The TUI is the primary interface but currently requires leaving the screen to halt trading or check if the signal loop / market feed is running.

## Scope
- Add `h` / `H` key to toggle TradingHalt on/off with a confirmation prompt (same pattern as close/reconcile)
- Add trading halt indicator to status row (🟢 ACTIVE / 🔴 HALTED)
- Add signal eval last-run timestamp to status row

## Acceptance Criteria
- `h` key prompts confirmation then halts/resumes trading and shows flash message

Tracks bd issue: coinbase_futures_bot-07m

title: Implement FuturesExecutor basis check via live REST mark price
description: FuturesExecutor#consider_entry has a TODO to query futures best bid/ask or mark price via REST to compute real basis bps. Currently it uses spot_price as a placeholder, so the basis gate never triggers meaningfully. Wire in CoinbaseRest or AdvancedTradeClient to fetch the mark/best-bid-ask for the target futures product.

title: Implement FuturesExecutor contract rollover logic
description: FuturesExecutor#perform_rollover has a TODO for the actual rollover logic. The contract detection and target resolution are present but the close-old / open-new order flow is not implemented. Should close positions in expiring contracts and re-open equivalent size on the target month contract.

title: Add observability: metrics and structured logging for order flow
description:
## Goal
Add consistent observability for order flow using one instrumentation seam, with structured logs and metrics for core trading events.

## Seam decision (required)
Use a dedicated observability module (adapter) that is called by execution/signal/reconciliation modules. Avoid ad-hoc direct logging/metrics in scattered callers.

## Required events
- `signal_fired`
- `order_placed`
- `order_rejected`
- `reconciliation_diff_detected`
- `execution_error`

## Required dimensions (where applicable)
- `trading_pair`
- `signal_side`
- `order_id`
- `mode` (paper/live)
- `reason` (reject/error)
- `latency_ms`

## Scope
- Structured logging (JSON-like fields or equivalent tagged structure)
- Metrics counters for required events
- Optional latency histogram/timer for execution path

## Files (expected touchpoints)
- `app/services/execution/futures_executor.rb`
- `app/jobs/generate_signals_job.rb`
- `app/services/trading/*`
- `config/initializers/*` (if needed for logger/metrics wiring)

## Non-goals
- Building dashboards/alerts in this issue.
- Replacing existing logging framework.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All required events emit structured logs with required dimensions.
- [ ] Metrics counters increment for required events.
- [ ] Instrumentation is routed through one observability seam (no copy/paste per caller).
- [ ] Tests verify emitted payload shape for at least signal, order placement, and error paths.

## Verification
- [ ] Run targeted specs for instrumented modules.
- [ ] Trigger one local sample flow and verify logs/metrics output format.

title: Add end-to-end integration spec for signal → executor flow
description: There are unit specs for strategies, signal alerts, and trading services individually, but no spec that exercises the full path: strategy emits signal → RealTimeSignalEvaluator persists it → GenerateSignalsJob reads it → FuturesExecutor places order. Add a VCR-cassette-backed integration spec covering the happy path and the kill-switch/paper-mode guard paths.

title: Make TUI primary interface
description:
I want our TUI to be our primary interface. This will require planning. 

The outcome of this ticket should be a plan to make this happen.

title: TUI
description:
Investigate if making an Ink (or Thor) based CLI/TLI makes sense. 

I think users would like operating the bot from the shell.

title: Wire runtime signal configuration to active TradingProfile
description:
## Summary
Update runtime signal/trading services to read from the active `TradingProfile` first, with env vars as fallback.

## Why
Profile data should actually drive behavior; otherwise UI/model work does not affect real signal generation and risk sizing.

## Scope
- Add `TradingConfiguration` service (or equivalent) to resolve effective settings
- Wire core paths (signal evaluator/jobs/chat sizing output) to resolved settings
- Keep env var fallback for safety and backward compatibility
- Add targeted specs for config resolution and service usage

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runtime components read from active profile when present
- Fallback to env defaults when no profile is active
- Existing behavior remains stable when profiles are absent
- Specs pass for profile + fallback paths

## Notes
This issue should be implemented after profile model foundation exists.

title: Add Trading Profile CRUD to operations UI
description:
## Summary
Add create/read/update/delete workflows for trading profiles in the web operations interface.

## Why
Operators need a safe, auditable way to manage profile parameters without editing scripts or environment variables manually.

## Scope
- Add operations UI screens/forms for profile CRUD
- Allow activating a profile from UI
- Server-side validation errors shown in UI
- Protect profile routes behind operations auth
- Add controller/view specs for CRUD flows

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can create, edit, delete, and activate profiles from UI
- Invalid profile input is rejected with clear errors
- Auth is required for all profile management routes
- CRUD specs pass

## Notes
Coordinate with profile model issue and authenticated operations issue.

title: Implement TradingProfile model and DB-backed profile presets
description:
## Summary
Introduce a `TradingProfile` model with migrations and seed data for preset configurations (e.g. conservative and 10-contract).

## Why
Risk/sizing settings should be explicit, versioned, and persisted instead of relying on duplicated shell scripts and mutable environment-only behavior.

## Scope
- Add `trading_profiles` table with key sizing/risk fields
- Add model validations and `active` profile semantics
- Add migration or seed step for default presets
- Add model specs for validation and active-profile behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- `TradingProfile` persists required runtime settings
- Exactly one active profile can be selected at a time
- Defaults are seeded for at least two profiles
- Model specs pass

## Out of Scope
- UI CRUD and runtime wiring (handled in separate issues)

title: Add authenticated operations UI for trading controls
description:
## Summary
Add a dedicated web operations page for runtime trading controls, protected by HTTP basic auth (same pattern as Positions UI).

## Why
Runtime controls are currently driven through shell commands and ad-hoc flows. A guarded UI path is safer and easier for routine operations.

## Scope
- Add `/operations` page with status display (trading active/emergency stop)
- Add actions for start, pause, and emergency stop
- Protect all operations routes with basic auth via env-configured credentials
- Reuse secure compare for auth checks
- Add controller + routing specs for auth and actions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unauthenticated requests to `/operations` are rejected
- Valid credentials allow access and actions
- Start/pause/emergency-stop actions update runtime state consistently
- Specs for controller auth and actions pass

## Notes
Keep this issue focused on authenticated controls only (no profile CRUD in this ticket).

title: Update README to reflect current project state and remove initial setup instructions.
description: Our readme describes this project, but it has not been worked on in a while.  Audit the codebase and We need a list of items to work on.

title: 🚀 CI Parallel Testing Implementation Complete
Optimize CI Pipeline with Parallel Testing
Implement real-time monitoring and trading readiness for BTC-USD and ETH-USD pairs
description:
## Overview
Set up real-time monitoring and trading readiness for the core trading pairs: BTC-USD and ETH-USD, with capacity to handle approximately 20 ETH contracts.

## Requirements
- **Trading Pairs**: Focus on BTC-USD and ETH-USD exclusively
- **Real-time Monitoring**: Continuous market data feeds for both pairs
- **Trading Capacity**: System should be ready to execute trades for ~20 ETH contracts
- **Market Data**: Real-time price feeds, order book data, and volume information
- **Execution Readiness**: Order placement capabilities and risk management for both pairs

## Technical Considerations
- Update market data subscribers to focus on BTC-USD and ETH-USD
- Ensure trading pair models support these specific instruments
- Implement position sizing calculations for ETH contract equivalents
- Set up real-time alerts and monitoring dashboards
- Configure risk limits appropriate for 20 ETH contract volume

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Real-time market data streaming for BTC-USD and ETH-USD
- [ ] Trading system ready to execute orders for both pairs
- [ ] Position management supports target contract volumes
- [ ] Risk controls calibrated for ETH contract equivalents
- [ ] Monitoring and alerting for both trading pairs

## Related Components
- Market data subscribers (`app/services/market_data/`)
- Trading pair models (`app/models/trading_pair.rb`)
- Position management (`app/models/position.rb`)
- Risk management services
- Real-time monitoring infrastructure

title: Project-wide review and documentation update
description:
## Overview
Conduct a comprehensive review of the entire coinbase_futures_bot project and update/create documentation in the docs folder to ensure all components are properly documented.

## Current Documentation Status
- Only `docs/candles.md` exists (covers candle data collection)
- Missing documentation for most project components

## Required Documentation Updates

### 1. Project Architecture Documentation
- [ ] Create `docs/architecture.md` - High-level system architecture, component relationships
- [ ] Create `docs/database-schema.md` - Database models, relationships, and migrations
- [ ] Create `docs/api-endpoints.md` - REST API documentation with examples

### 2. Core Services Documentation
- [ ] Create `docs/services/` directory structure
- [ ] Document market data services (Coinbase subscribers, REST client)
- [ ] Document trading services (futures executor, positions management)
- [ ] Document strategy services (signal generation, backtesting)
- [ ] Document sentiment analysis services

### 3. Job System Documentation
- [ ] Create `docs/jobs.md` - Background job system overview
- [ ] Document individual job responsibilities and scheduling
- [ ] Document GoodJob configuration and monitoring

### 4. Configuration and Environment
- [ ] Create `docs/configuration.md` - Environment variables, configuration options
- [ ] Document Coinbase API setup and authentication
- [ ] Document database configuration and connection management

### 5. Development and Testing
- [ ] Create `docs/development.md` - Setup instructions, development workflow
- [ ] Create `docs/testing.md` - Testing strategy, running tests, test data
- [ ] Document Rake tasks and their purposes

### 6. Deployment and Operations
- [ ] Create `docs/deployment.md` - Deployment instructions, environment setup
- [ ] Create `docs/monitoring.md` - Health checks, logging, observability
- [ ] Document Docker setup and containerization

### 7. Trading Strategy Documentation
- [ ] Create `docs/strategies.md` - Available trading strategies and their logic
- [ ] Document backtesting framework and results
- [ ] Document paper trading system

## Review Tasks

### Code Quality Assessment
- [ ] Review code organization and structure
- [ ] Identify areas for refactoring or improvement
- [ ] Check for consistent patterns and conventions
- [ ] Review error handling and logging

### Security Review
- [ ] Review authentication and authorization
- [ ] Check for sensitive data exposure
- [ ] Review API key management
- [ ] Assess rate limiting and abuse prevention

### Performance Review
- [ ] Analyze database query performance
- [ ] Review job scheduling and execution
- [ ] Check for memory leaks or resource issues
- [ ] Assess API rate limit handling

### Testing Coverage
- [ ] Review test coverage across components
- [ ] Identify missing test scenarios
- [ ] Check test data quality and fixtures
- [ ] Review integration test coverage

## Deliverables
1. Comprehensive project review report
2. Updated/created documentation files
3. List of identified issues and improvement opportunities
4. Recommendations for code quality and architecture improvements

## Priority
High - Documentation is essential for project maintainability and onboarding new developers

## Labels
- `documentation`
- `review`
- `quality`
- `architecture`
- `enhancement`

title: Enable branch protection on main
description:
Please enable branch protection on `main` with the following settings:

- Require a pull request before merging
  - Require approvals: 1
  - Dismiss stale approvals when new commits are pushed
  - Require review from Code Owners (optional)
- Require status checks to pass before merging
  - Required checks: CI / RuboCop (lint), CI / Brakeman (security)
- Require signed commits (optional)
- Require linear history (optional)
- Do not allow bypassing the above rules

Also consider enabling Dependabot security updates for Ruby gems.

Refs: #security #process

title: Enable branch protection on main
description:
Please enable branch protection on `main` with the following settings:

- Require a pull request before merging
  - Require approvals: 1
  - Dismiss stale reviews: enabled
  - Require review from Code Owners: enabled
- Require status checks to pass before merging
  - Status checks: CI (Rubocop, Brakeman)
  - Require branches to be up to date before merging: enabled
- Require signed commits: enabled (optional, recommended)
- Include administrators: enabled
- Restrict who can push to matching branches: only via PR (no direct pushes)

Once applied, comment here with a confirmation.

title: Persist CalibrationJob best params to a settings store
description:
## Goal
Persist `CalibrationJob` best parameters so runtime strategy modules can load calibrated values instead of relying on hardcoded defaults.

## Seam decision (required)
Introduce one configuration module seam for calibrated strategy params:
- Interface: read/write calibrated params by symbol + strategy key
- Implementation: backed by a dedicated DB table/model (preferred for auditability)

## Data model requirements
- Key fields: `symbol`, `strategy_key`, `tp_target`, `sl_target`, `calibrated_at`
- Optional metadata: `source_job_id`, `window_start`, `window_end`
- Unique index on (`symbol`, `strategy_key`)

## Scope
1. Migration + model for calibrated settings
2. `CalibrationJob` writes best params through config seam
3. Strategy runtime reads through same seam with fallback defaults
4. Specs for write, update, read, and fallback behavior

## Non-goals
- Changing calibration algorithm/grid-search logic.
- Building admin UI for calibrated settings.

## Files (expected touchpoints)
- `db/migrate/*_create_*calibrated*params*.rb` (name may vary)
- `app/models/*`
- `app/jobs/calibration_job.rb`
- `app/services/strategy/*`
- `spec/jobs/calibration_job_spec.rb` and related strategy specs

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Calibration output persists to DB via one config seam.
- [ ] Strategies load persisted values at runtime.
- [ ] Missing calibrated rows cleanly fall back to defaults.
- [ ] Specs cover create/update/read/fallback behavior.
- [ ] Migration is reversible and schema constraints are in place.

## Verification
- [ ] Run migration locally.
- [ ] Run targeted job + strategy specs.
- [ ] Confirm persisted values are read by strategy path.

title: Convert remaining buy/sell signal consumers to long/short at API boundary
description: SignalAlert.create_entry_signal! now normalizes buy→long / sell→short, but other consumers of raw signal side strings (e.g. strategy outputs, executor calls) may still pass buy/sell. Audit all callers and ensure long/short are the canonical values end-to-end so the side normalizer is not needed at runtime.
close_reason: Strategies emit :long/:short; SignalAlert canonicalizes on save; simulator/Position boundaries use SignalSide; dedupe matches legacy buy/sell rows.

title: Documentation
description:
## Goal
Produce operator-facing and user-facing documentation that explains how to use the bot safely for market analysis, trade execution, and position monitoring.

## Scope (must include)
1. **Getting started flow**
   - Required environment/config
   - Local start commands
   - Safety disclaimers (paper mode / kill switch)
2. **Market analysis flow**
   - How data is ingested
   - How signals are generated and interpreted
3. **Trade execution flow**
   - How signals reach execution
   - Guards (paper mode, kill switch, position limits)
4. **Position monitoring flow**
   - Where to view active positions and status
   - How reconciliation/closure is handled
5. **Operational runbook**
   - Health checks and key logs
   - What to do on failure (API/auth/job issues)

## Non-goals
- No new product features.
- No UI redesign.
- No architecture refactor in this issue.

## Files
- `README.md`
- `docs/development.md`
- `docs/api-endpoints.md`
- `docs/services/trading.md`
- `docs/architecture.md`
- Add any missing docs under `docs/` as needed.

## Deliverable structure
- Add a central “How to use the bot” entry section in `README.md` that links to dedicated docs pages.
- Keep each workflow in its own section with command examples and expected outcomes.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A new user can follow docs to run the bot locally and verify health.
- [ ] Docs clearly describe analyze → signal → execute → monitor path.
- [ ] Safety guards and risk controls are documented in one place.
- [ ] Troubleshooting section includes common failure modes and recovery steps.
- [ ] Documentation links are internally consistent and not broken.

## Verification
- [ ] Manually follow documented local setup steps from a clean shell.
- [ ] Validate all referenced commands and paths exist.

title: Clean up stringio gem ambiguity warning in Ruby environment
description: A repeated stringio gem ambiguity warning appears during RSpec runs (noted in session logs). Identify whether it comes from Gemfile, a transitive dep, or the RVM gemset, and resolve it so test output is clean.

title: Refactor remaining receive_message_chain specs to use real records
description: Several spec files still use heavy receive_message_chain doubles that make tests brittle and hard to maintain. Continue the pattern established in health_check and realtime specs: use real DB records and real service objects where cheap, replacing chains of mocked relations.
